{{Infobox concept
| name = Naraka Traveler
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Naraka Traveler Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] are individuals who draw power from the [[Territory]] of [[Naraka]], also known as the Cavern of Flame. This realm is characterized by themes of law and order, which often compels its [[Travelers]] to act as agents of justice or to uphold high moral principles. They are frequently identified by their red robes and a glowing red rune or brand located on their palms, which they use to perform intricate patterns to summon forces from their [[Territory]]. These summons can include various entities, such as burning insects or ash hounds. To prepare for their abilities, initiates undergo a ritual involving the consumption of fruit from an obsidian tree, which provides varying degrees of protection against extreme heat. This protection is vital for managing their powers without sustaining injury. The process of becoming a [[Traveler]] requires the act of [[Traveling]]. While they possess significant capabilities, using their summons can cause them physical pain. [[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] also play roles in navigating the world through the use of [[Naraka]] [[Gates]], which can be held open by multiple [[Travelers]] working in coordination.}}

== House of Blades ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Naraka Traveler]] can have a glowing red rune on their hand.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] eat a fruit to protect them from heat during training.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] are a judgmental group of individuals, including [[Alin]] and the granddaughter of [[Grandmaster Naraka]], who possess empty blue eyes and specific tattoos on their hands. These people are capable of remaining cool and dry in intense heat, enduring mere pain, and summoning burning insects from thin air. While they guard [[Grandmaster Naraka]]'s door through a dedicated roped-off section, they also possess the power to take others to [[Myria]] or evaporate serpents using a red-marked hand. Despite these abilities, the group has faced violence from [[Simon]], who murdered several of their members.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">13</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Naraka Traveler]] had empty blue eyes.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Naraka]]'s granddaughter was a [[Naraka Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] call burning insects out of thin air.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] murdered several [[Naraka]] [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] have a specific symbol tattooed on their hands.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] are a group that tends to be judgmental.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] guard [[Grandmaster Naraka]]'s door.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A robed [[Naraka Traveler]] is present in the room and can make serpents evaporate with a red-marked hand.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|There is a roped-off section for [[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] to enter and exit.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] are people who can remain cool and dry in heat.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] are accustomed to mere pain.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Naraka Traveler]] can take someone to [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is a [[Naraka Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] serve as guards stationed at the tent and are distinguished by their red robes. Their primary duty involves holding the [[Naraka Gate]] open for passage. While it is noted that [[Cynara]] possessed the qualities to be a wonderful member of this group, they ultimately follow the direct commands of [[Leah]], who orders them to open a Gate.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] hold the [[Naraka Gate]] open.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cynara]] would have made a wonderful [[Naraka Traveler]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] wear red robes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Naraka Traveler]] is a guard at the tent.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] orders the [[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] to open a Gate.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The Naraka Traveler leaned forward and whispered, “They didn’t catch you the first time, but everyone has heard the story by now. They deserved the chance to thank you.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“Open a Gate!” Leah ordered the Naraka Travelers, marching forward with Simon and the other two on her heels.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate concepts]]
[[Category:Titles]]
